headlines.title: Member of Vance’s Secret Service detail removed amid probe into alleged compromise of security info
headlines.description: A member of JD Vance’s protective detail has been removed pending an investigation into allegations of compromising the vice president’s security, a Secret Service spokesman said Thursday.

headlines.title: Oil price surge as war with Iran expands
headlines.description: International oil prices pushed past $100 a barrel today as markets reacted to a new threat by the Houthis in Yemen against ships in the Red Sea. NBC News’ Chief Foreign Correspondent Richard Engel reports.

headlines.title: Secret Service agent from Vice President Vance detail removed
headlines.description: The agent is being investigated following a media report that described some agents as being “fed up” with last minute travel and changing schedules. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell reports.

headlines.title: President Trump announces new tariffs on dozens of countries
headlines.description: President Trump announced new 10% to 12.5% tariffs on 60 countries over accusations of "forced labor." Those new duties will take effect at 12:01 a.m. ET on Friday morning.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul explains why she signed the first statewide moratorium on data centers
headlines.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul said she felt the need to sign the nation’s first statewide moratorium on data center development because tech giants “are flooding the zone” with construction efforts faster than states and localities can keep up.

headlines.title: U.S. sets new tariffs on dozens of countries as temporary duties are set to expire
headlines.description: The U.S. Trade Rep announced a wave of tariffs on 60 trade partners in order to replace President Donald Trump’s temporary tariff which expires at midnight.

headlines.title: Justice Department agrees to withdraw subpoenas of New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
headlines.description: A judge repeatedly questioned why prosecutors had issued the subpoenas before pursuing alternative investigative steps.

headlines.title: Oil prices surge as Congress splits on Iran war votes: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, we examine how the escalating Iran war is playing out in the oil markets and the halls of the Capitol. Plus, Scott Wong and Matt Dixon have a profile of the little-known new senator from South Carolina.

headlines.title: Rep. Jim Jordan formally asks DOJ to prosecute Jack Smith over congressional testimony
headlines.description: Jordan, chair of the House Judiciary Committee, accused Smith of lying in his testimony before the committee in December. Smith’s lawyers denied he made any false statements.

headlines.title: House votes to end Iran war, but Senate Republicans vote to let Trump continue waging it
headlines.description: The House approved a nonbinding proposal to end the Iran war, directing President Donald Trump to remove armed forces from hostilities.

headlines.title: Arizona Democrats reject party leaders’ pick for a key battleground district
headlines.description: Democrat Amish Shah, a physician and former state lawmaker, has opened a lead in the primary for a key Arizona battleground district even though party leaders backed another candidate.
